steps.title: Factor the denominator g(x)
steps.content: Find two numbers that multiply to $-3$ and add to $-2$: those are $-3$ and $+1$. $$x^2 - 2x - 3 = (x-3)(x+1)$$

steps.title: Cancel the common factor
steps.content: Since $x > 3$, we have $x - 3 \neq 0$, so the factor cancels: $$\frac{x(x-3)(x+3)}{(x-3)(x+1)} = \frac{x(x+3)}{x+1}$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• A: $\frac{1}{x+1}$ — Lost both $x$ and $(x+3)$ from the numerator. Eliminated.
  • B: $\frac{x+3}{x+1}$ — Missing the factor of $x$ in the numerator. Eliminated.
  • C: $\frac{x(x-3)}{x+1}$ — The $(x-3)$ factor cancels with the denominator; it should not appear in the result. Eliminated.
  • D: $\frac{x(x+3)}{x+1}$ — After canceling $(x-3)$, this is exactly what remains. That matches choice D — D is correct.
